Imports

../application, ../controller, paths, autolink, router

Types

HttpRouteMeta = object
  path*, regex*: string
  description*: string
  public*: bool = true
HttpRoutes = CritBitTree[HttpRouteVerbs]
This table is used to return an index of all routes at GET / request 
HttpRouteVerbs = CritBitTree[HttpRouteMeta]
HttpService = object
When the service type is either a UnixService or WebService. By default the generated HttpService is always private and cannot be accessed without an API key 
HttpServiceIndex = ref object

Used to generate a readonly JSON Index available at GET / request.

Provides meta data for available routes, authorization type, service version and compilation time.

ServiceProvider = object
  name*, description*, author*, version*: string
ServiceType = enum
  Global = "Global",        ## Defines a Global service as part of the main application
  Singleton = "Singleton",  ## Defines a Singleton service as part of the main application
  ChannelService = "Channel", ## Thread-based Service enabling inter thread communications 
  WebService = "WebService", ## REST API Service Provider that connects over TCP/IP
  ThreadService = "ThreadService" ## Defines thread-based `WebService` part of the main application
Defines Supranim Service types 
SingletonService = object

Vars

index {.compileTime.} = genSym(nskVar, "IndexRoute")
routerInstance {.compileTime.} = genSym(nskVar, "RouterInstance")

Consts

ServiceManagerThreadServices = r"ServiceManagerThreadServices"
ServiceManagerThreadServicesClients = r"ServiceManagerThreadServicesClients"

Macros

macro extractThreadServicesBackend()
Extracts the generated client-side API for the ThreadService and ChannelService and saves it in a runtime folder ({app}/.runtime/). The generated code is then available to the main application via import supranim/runtime 
macro extractThreadServicesClient()
Extracts the generated client-side API for the ThreadService and ChannelService and saves it in a runtime folder ({app}/.runtime/). The generated code is then available to the main application via import supranim/runtime

Templates

template initChannelRouter()
Initialize the router for the channel-based service. The API provided by the channel-based service is similar to the one provided by the web service. The only difference is that the channel-based service does not require a web server. 
template initRouter(): untyped
template initService(id, config: untyped)

Create a new Supranim Service. Supported service types:

Singleton Services are built within the main app providing a singleton pattern based on pkg/threading/once.

ChannelService Services are thread-based services that can be used for inter-thread communication

UnixService and WebService Services are REST API microservices wrapped by the built-in web server based on pkg/httbeast

https://dev.to/vearutop/using-nginx-as-a-proxy-to-multiple-unix-sockets-3c7a

Exports

send, getCode, getRequestBody, keypair, go, redirectTo, send, setBody, getAgent, getFieldsJson, getHeaders, params, redirect301, redirectUri, getIp, addHeader, getSessionCookie, getHeaders, getSomeBodyData, respond, ctrl, setCode, getContentType, sendJson, getQuery, getFieldsTableJson, getUriPath, resp, redirect, storage, getCookies, setParams, go, emptyResponseBody, getUrl, hasCookies, getHeader, getClientData, json, getQueryTable, getBody, BodyData, Response, send, $, getHttpMethod, getFieldsTable, getPlatform, findHeader, getBodyDataJson, SomeBodyData, getClientId, getBrowserName, getFields, respond, getDefault, newController, getBodyData, isAuth, Request, getBody, streamFile, toString
